differentials.name: Astrocytoma
differentials.raw: Astrocytoma (hypodense, displaces 4th ventricle from midline, cystic lucency, intramedullary)

context: =  in majority benign slow-growing neoplasm of mature well-differentiated ependymal cells lining the ventricles Incidence:   most common in children; 5-9% of all primary CNS neoplasms; 15% of posterior fossa tumors in children; 63% of spinal intramedullary gliomas Histo:   benign aggregates of ependymocytes in form of perivascular pseudorosettes; may have papillary pattern (difficult DDx to choroid plexus papilloma) Age:  (a) supratentorial: at any age (atrium / foramen of Monro) (b)  posterior fossa: age peaks at 5 + 34 years; M÷F = 0.8÷1 Associated with:   neurofibromatosis Location: (a)  in
